published.text:
### Existing page role retained
Heading: AI Automation for Pest Control Companies
Body: The page still acts as the broad commercial parent for pest-control automation — urgent response, recurring-service reminders, seasonal campaigns, quote follow-up, review workflows, and phone coverage.
Sub: The refresh does not turn it into a new page type. It makes the parent page more useful once the owner needs to choose which narrower workflow deserves the first build.

### Hero framing tightened around first-project selection
Section: Updated hero support copy
- The hero subtext now makes the page answer a sharper buyer question:
  - which leak should usually be fixed first
  - where ROI tends to show up first
  - when a lighter follow-up layer is enough
  - when a heavier live-answering system becomes justified
- Purpose: stop the page from reading like a generic list of automations and make it easier for search/AI systems to extract the real decision frame.

### New first-project routing block
Section: Choose the first pest-control workflow by where revenue is leaking
- Adds a new answer-extractable card section that separates five distinct first-project choices:
  - urgent lead follow-up
  - missed-call recovery
  - recurring reminders and renewals
  - seasonal campaigns
  - heavier live AI phone answering
- Each card uses explicit route references to live child pages so the parent now sends readers and crawlers into:
  - /urgent-lead-follow-up-for-pest-control-companies
  - /missed-call-text-back-for-pest-control-companies
  - /recurring-service-reminder-automation-for-pest-control-companies
  - /seasonal-campaign-automation-for-pest-control-companies
  - /ai-phone-answering-for-pest-control-companies
- Purpose: keep the broad parent from acting like a dead-end category page after the owner identifies the leak.

### Internal-link cluster tightened
Section: Parent-to-child crawl paths
- The proof-links block now routes into:
  - /what-to-automate-first-for-pest-control-companies
  - /urgent-lead-follow-up-for-pest-control-companies
  - /recurring-service-reminder-automation-for-pest-control-companies
  - /case-studies
- Bottom related links now stay inside the live pest-control cluster instead of jumping mostly to generic or adjacent-industry pages.
- New related links emphasize:
  - the first-project selector
  - urgent lead follow-up
  - recurring reminders
  - seasonal campaigns
  - quote follow-up
  - AI phone answering

### FAQ extraction strengthened
Section: New buyer question
- Added a new FAQ answer for: What should a pest control company automate first?
- The answer mirrors the page’s new routing logic and explicitly distinguishes between:
  - fresh-lead response problems
  - missed-call problems
  - renewal / retention problems
  - seasonal campaign timing problems
  - heavier live-answering fit
- Purpose: give search/AI systems a compact direct answer instead of forcing them to infer the recommendation from multiple sections.

### llm / llms summaries refreshed
Section: External discovery wording
- Updated the pest-control parent summary in both public/llm.txt and public/llms.txt.
- New summary explains that the page now helps owners choose the right first workflow by leak instead of presenting one generic automation stack.

edits.description: This was a bounded indexing / internal-linking refresh on an existing industry parent page, not a net-new page ship.
Tone stays practical: follow the leak, keep the first build narrow, and only move up to heavier phone automation when the lighter layer is clearly no longer enough.
No new proof claims or fake pest-control case-study claims were added.
The update is mostly about cleaner buyer framing, answer extraction, and stronger parent-to-child routing inside the live pest-control cluster.
